checkpoint_state: post-lambda / post-sigma-b / post-safe-phi-b / post-upsilon-a / post-phi-b3 / post-upsilon-b / pre-phi-b4-or-other-next-block

hotswap_boundary_reconsideration.summary: Completed Φ-B3 plus Υ-B0..Υ-B2 make Φ-B4 judgeable with much less ambiguity, but do not make hotswap safe to begin.

distributed_authority_reconsideration.summary: Distributed authority still lacks authority handoff, quorum, distributed replay verification, and trust-bearing execution maturity.

further_release_control_operational_matu.summary: The repo now has explicit coexistence, parity, receipt, and execution-envelope law, which supports a further narrow Υ band focused on rehearsal and rollback maturity.

zeta_blocker_reduction.summary: This band closes major doctrinal ambiguity, but Ζ remains blocked by hotswap boundaries, distributed authority, release rehearsal execution maturity, and trust-bearing execution gaps.

ambiguities_eliminated: completed phi-b3 plus upsilon-b0..upsilon-b2 does not automatically clear phi-b4
completed phi-b3 plus upsilon-b0..upsilon-b2 does not place phi-b5 on the same readiness footing as phi-b4
reduced release-ops ambiguity does not equal live-cutover maturity

risky_phi_b_readiness.title: HOTSWAP_BOUNDARIES-0
risky_phi_b_readiness.summary: Hotswap remains dangerous because hot-swappable renderers, live protocol upgrades, and partial live module reload still require new runtime foundation even after coexistence and release-ops doctrine hardening.

risky_phi_b_readiness.title: DISTRIBUTED_AUTHORITY_FOUNDATIONS-0
risky_phi_b_readiness.summary: Distributed authority remains premature because distributed shard relocation is still unrealistic currently, while authority handoff, proof-anchor quorum, and trust-bearing execution remain unresolved.

further_upsilon_readiness.title: RELEASE_REHEARSAL_SANDBOX_AND_PROOF_BACKED_ROLLBACK_ALIGNMENT-0
further_upsilon_readiness.summary: Proof-backed rollback is ready now in foundation evidence and release rehearsal is foundation-ready but not implemented, making this the next safest blocker reducer after Υ-B2.

further_upsilon_readiness.title: CANARY_AND_DETERMINISTIC_DOWNGRADE_EXECUTION_ALIGNMENT-0
further_upsilon_readiness.summary: Canary and deterministic downgrade execution are plausible next-band work, but should remain downstream of the rehearsal and rollback alignment prompt.

further_upsilon_readiness.title: PUBLICATION_TRUST_EXECUTION_PREREQUISITE_ALIGNMENT-0
further_upsilon_readiness.summary: Publication remains gated, trust policy remains provisional, and trust roots remain empty, so this family is still too dangerous to operationalize next.

further_upsilon_readiness.title: LIVE_TRUST_ROOT_ROTATION_PREREQUISITES-0
further_upsilon_readiness.summary: Live trust-root rotation remains blocked by provisional trust policies and empty trust-root state.

zeta_blockers_or_prerequisites.summary: Restartless replacement, partial reload, and live protocol evolution still require explicit safe boundaries.
Authority handoff, distributed replay verification, quorum semantics, and shard relocation remain unresolved.
The doctrine exists, but production-like rehearsal and proof-backed rollback orchestration remain operationally incomplete.
Deterministic downgrade and staged rollout remain more explicit than before, but still need further operational alignment.
Publication is still gated, trust policy is provisional, and trust roots remain empty.
Trust-root rotation remains explicitly dangerous and under-founded for live execution.
The doctrine stack is explicit, but controlled live-cutover proof is still absent.
Receipts and provenance continuity are now doctrinally explicit, but operational continuity for live-cutover and revocation-class flows remains partial.

must_avoid_replacing: canonical parity, receipt, and execution-envelope semantics
canonical publication, trust, and licensing gate semantics
derived mirrors or tool wrappers promoted into execution authority

ownership_projection_cautions: fields/ remains canonical semantic field substrate while field/ remains transitional
schema/ remains canonical semantic contract law while schemas/ remains a projection or validator-facing mirror
packs/ remains canonical for runtime packaging and activation scope while data/packs/ remains scoped authored-pack authority
canonical prose and semantic specs outrank projected or generated mirrors
the thin runtime/ root is not canonical by name alone
release and control-plane convenience must not infer canon, permission, or runtime safety
older planning numbering drift remains evidence rather than authority

final_verdict.reason: The completed band materially lowers ambiguity but still leaves Φ-B4 dangerous and Φ-B5 premature. A narrow further Υ block around release rehearsal and rollback maturity is the safest next blocker reducer.

notes_on_identifier_policy: Stable family identifiers are preferred over stale older Υ numbering in legacy planning mirrors.
Υ-C seed labels in this checkpoint are local planning labels anchored to repo-grounded family identifiers.
